---
title: Full pipeline
---

This tutorial runs the pipeline the engines will plug into: specify parameters, sample draws, evaluate a model with `run_psa`, then feed the outcomes to CEA and VoI. It assumes the [bring your own outputs tutorial](byo-outputs.qmd), which covers the analysis functions in more detail. Here the focus is the run loop and its contract.

## Specify and sample parameters

Distributions are specified directly or from published mean and standard error. `ParameterSet.sample` returns a draw matrix whose index is the canonical iteration index; a Spearman rank correlation can be requested per parameter pair.

```{python}
from heormodel.params import Beta, Gamma, Normal, ParameterSet
from heormodel.run import SeedManager

params = ParameterSet(
    {
        "p_event": Beta.from_mean_se(0.20, 0.03),
        "rr_treat": Normal(0.70, 0.10),
        "c_event": Gamma.from_mean_se(25_000, 3_000),
        "c_treat": Gamma.from_mean_se(4_000, 500),
        "u_event": Beta.from_mean_se(0.65, 0.05),
    }
)
seeds = SeedManager(42)
draws = params.sample(4_000, seed=seeds.generator())
draws.head(3).round(3)
```

## A model as a callable

An engine is anything that maps draws to `Outcomes`. A plain function satisfying `ModelFn` is enough: here, a two-strategy decision tree over one year. `Outcomes.from_wide` assembles the schema from per-strategy cost and effect columns.

```{python}
import pandas as pd
from heormodel.models import Outcomes

def decision_tree(d: pd.DataFrame) -> Outcomes:
    p_treated = d["p_event"] * d["rr_treat"]
    costs = pd.DataFrame({
        "No treatment": d["p_event"] * d["c_event"],
        "Treatment": d["c_treat"] + p_treated * d["c_event"],
    })
    effects = pd.DataFrame({
        "No treatment": 1 - d["p_event"] * (1 - d["u_event"]),
        "Treatment": 1 - p_treated * (1 - d["u_event"]),
    })
    return Outcomes.from_wide(costs, effects)
```

## Run the PSA

`run_psa` evaluates the model and guarantees the outcomes carry exactly the draws' index, preserving the parameter/outcome linkage VoI needs. It runs in parallel over all cores by default, printing a time-remaining readout when `stderr` is a terminal. This tutorial's run is tiny, so it passes `sequential=True` to run in-process and stay quiet.

```{python}
from heormodel.run import run_psa

outcomes = run_psa(decision_tree, draws, sequential=True)
outcomes.summary().round(3)
```

## CEA and VoI on the same objects

```{python}
import numpy as np
from heormodel.cea import icer_table
from heormodel.voi import evpi, evppi_ranking

icer_table(outcomes).round(3)
```

```{python}
WTP = 50_000.0
print(f"EVPI at WTP {WTP:,.0f}: {evpi(outcomes, WTP):,.2f}")
evppi_ranking(outcomes, draws, WTP).round(2)
```

## Convergence check

`running_means` tracks the running mean of an outcome column per strategy as iterations accumulate; flat traces at the right edge suggest the iteration count is adequate for the means (VoI estimates typically need more).

```{python}
from heormodel.run import running_means

running_means(outcomes).plot(xlabel="iterations", ylabel="mean cost");
```

The [engines](../concepts/engines.qmd) concept page explains the protocol this callable satisfied. Next: the [value of information](voi.qmd) tutorial takes outcomes like these through EVPI, EVPPI, and EVSI, checking each against a closed form.
